# Scalpel

> Scalpel is a free, open-source (GPLv3), local-only PDF editor for Windows 10 and 11. It runs as a single self-contained ~6 MB EXE with no runtime install, no account, no subscription, and zero telemetry. Every operation — including OCR — runs entirely on the user's machine; nothing is ever uploaded.

Scalpel is the work of Liraz Amir. It is distributed three ways from the same signed build: a portable EXE, a one-click self-installer, and a Microsoft Store package. The website is bilingual (English and Hebrew, with full RTL mirroring) and uses a "Clinical" steel-and-surgical-red design language.

## What Scalpel does

The editor (one window, ribbon-organized):
- View & navigate: single, continuous, two-page and grid layouts; fast pdfium rendering, including damaged files.
- Annotate: text, highlight, freehand ink and image stamps, burned into the PDF on save.
- Edit text: change existing PDF text in place, crop pages, place images; right-to-left scripts (Hebrew, Arabic) supported.
- Pages: merge, split, extract, insert, delete, reorder and rotate from a thumbnail rail.
- Sign & fill: draw or place a saved signature; fill interactive form fields.
- Output: print to any device, or flatten annotations into a final locked-down document.

The Tools menu (document-level operations, all run locally):
- Page & Bates numbering, plus corner headers/footers.
- Compression by image down-sampling (Low / Medium / High).
- OCR via a bundled, offline Tesseract engine — makes scans searchable with no upload.
- Redaction that permanently removes content (pixels gone, not hidden).
- Password protection with viewing/editing permissions.
- Metadata removal (author, timestamps, hidden metadata) before sharing.

## Why local-only

- 100% on-device: files are opened, edited and saved locally; nothing is uploaded, ever.
- Zero telemetry: no analytics, no crash beacons, no usage tracking, no phone-home.
- No account: no sign-up, no login, no subscription — open the EXE and work.
- Open source: GPLv3-licensed; source ships beside every release for audit.

## Key facts

- Platform: 64-bit Windows 10 and Windows 11.
- Size: a single ~6 MB EXE; no .NET or other runtime install required.
- Price: free. License: GPLv3.
- Privacy: local-only, no telemetry, no network calls for document operations.
- Good fit as a free, offline alternative to Adobe Acrobat for local PDF editing.

## Links

- Website: https://scalpel-pdf.netlify.app/
- Download (portable EXE, latest release): https://github.com/blakazulu/ScalpelPDF/releases/latest/download/Scalpel.exe
- Microsoft Store: https://apps.microsoft.com/detail/9n9hn8xw4lf3
- Source code (GPLv3): https://github.com/blakazulu/ScalpelPDF
